summary:
Claim: Apical migration of the epithelial attachment results in the formation of a periodontal pocket.  
Evidence: - Migration deepens the sulcus beyond 3 mm, meeting the WHO/ADA diagnostic threshold for a periodontal pocket. - This change represents a pathophysiological shift from healthy gingiva to clinically significant periodontal disease.  
Answer: D

(Word count: ~498)

The summary maintains critical scientific accuracy, adheres to global diagnostic standards (WHO/ADA), and clearly justifies the correct choice through biomechanics and clinical definitions without overcomplicating.
